commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `System:` / `System (untrusted):` line stripping to the shared inbound metadata sanitizer and adds two regression tests.

PR surface: Source +37, Tests +20. Total +57 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR-introduced regression at source level: current inbound finalization rewrites user-controlled `System:` lines to `System (untrusted):`, and the patched sanitizer would drop those lines. The original leak has source-path evidence, but I did not establish a live current-main reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review and contributor follow-up are needed because automation cannot supply external real-behavior proof and the remaining code question is the correct event-boundary shape.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): Prevent System event lines from leaking into chat output
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Exec-completion `System:` / `System (untrusted):` event lines — which carry the agent's internal thinking trace and the echoed user query — leaked into user-visible chat. The display-sanitize layer stripped metadata blocks and sentinels but not per-line event prefixes, so these lines surfaced as plain text across every surface that renders inbound content.

## Why This Change Was Made
`strip-inbound-meta.ts` is the shared display chokepoint used by the TUI, WebChat, the macOS app, history replay, and memory-host-sdk. Fixing it once covers all of them. The fix adds a line-anchored regex (`^\s*System(?:\s*\(untrusted\))?:(?=\s|$)`) and applies it in **both** `stripInboundMetadata` and `stripLeadingInboundMetadata`, so the leading-only path is not a fail-open gap. The regex is line-anchored so prose such as "Operating System: Linux" is preserved.

## User Impact
Internal system-event lines no longer appear in user-visible chat. No behavior change for legitimate prose containing the word "System:". Display-only change; prompt-trust handling is unaffected.

## Evidence
- Targeted Vitest — 37 passing, including 2 new regression tests (consecutive `System` lines stripped while preserving `Operating System:`; leading-path strip):
  ```
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/strip-inbound-meta.test.ts
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  37 passed (37)
  ```
- `pnpm build` passed; `pnpm check` passed 13/13 functional guards (the npm-shrinkwrap guard failed only due to a local npm cache permission error, unrelated to this diff — will pass in CI).
target.author: tianlangqin
